component.name: duckdb
component.title: DuckDB
component.description: Interact with DuckDB, the in-process analytical SQL database, for embedded analytics workloads.
component.label: database,bigdata

databasePath.description: Embedded database path (:memory: or a file path). Used when jdbcUrl is not set.

dataSource.description: Shared JDBC DataSource for all endpoints.

jdbcUrl.description: Full JDBC URL override (for example jdbc:duckdb:/path/to/db). When set, databasePath is ignored.

lazyStartProducer.label: producer
lazyStartProducer.description: Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.

autowiredEnabled.label: advanced
autowiredEnabled.description: Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc.

CamelDuckDbOperation.description: Overrides the operation configured on the endpoint.

CamelDuckDbDatabasePath.description: Overrides the database path configured on the endpoint.

CamelDuckDbTable.description: Overrides the target table configured on the endpoint.

CamelDuckDbQuery.description: Overrides the SQL for query or execute operations.

CamelDuckDbRowsWritten.description: The number of rows written or affected.

CamelDuckDbRowsRead.description: The number of rows returned by a query.

CamelDuckDbPingOk.description: The boolean result of a ping operation.

databasePath.description: Database path (:memory: or a file path). An optional /table segment sets the default table.

batchSize.description: Batch size for insert when the body is a List. 0 inserts all rows in one JDBC batch.

format.description: File format for copy: csv, parquet, json or auto (inferred from the file extension).

jdbcUrl.description: Full JDBC URL override. When set, databasePath is not used to build the URL.

operation.description: The operation to perform: execute, query, insert, copy or ping.

query.description: Static SQL for query when the message body is empty.

readOnly.description: Open the embedded database file in read-only mode when supported.

resultFormat.description: How query results are returned: LIST_MAP (List of Map) or JSON array string.

table.description: Target table for insert and copy operations. Can also be set in the path as databasePath/table or overridden with the CamelDuckDbTable header.

lazyStartProducer.label: producer,advanced
lazyStartProducer.description: Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.
